vBRIEFInfo.author: overdeck/0.0.0
vBRIEFInfo.description: Plan for PAN-1864: add the missing AC4 regression test for deterministic deacon-side review synthesis (the fix itself already shipped on main)

plan.title: Regression test for deterministic deacon-side review synthesis (synthesizeReviewFromReports)
plan.author: agent:claude-opus-4-8

narratives.Problem: PAN-1864 asked for deterministic deacon-side review synthesis so a wedged convoy parent can never block the pipeline. That fix ALREADY shipped and was verified on origin/main (commit bb57e9f16; flywheel log c73f74f33). All four functional acceptance criteria are met in current code: synthesizeReviewFromReports (src/lib/cloister/deacon.ts:5939) plus the wedge-detection/cleanup call site (deacon.ts:6068-6140). The ONLY unmet acceptance criterion is AC4 — the unit test. No test anywhere references synthesizeReviewFromReports, findBlockingFindings, or DeaconReviewSynthesis, so the critical deterministic-synthesis path has zero regression coverage.
narratives.Proposal: Add a single new vitest file, src/lib/cloister/__tests__/deacon-review-synthesis.test.ts, that imports the pure exported function synthesizeReviewFromReports (behind the same vi.mock import scaffold the sibling deacon-stash-janitor.test.ts already uses) and asserts the correct verdict / topBlocker / body for representative on-disk reviewer reports — with no live parent and no filesystem. Touch no production code.
narratives.NonGoals:
- Do NOT modify synthesizeReviewFromReports, findBlockingFindings, the wedge-detection call site (deacon.ts:6068-6140), or any other production code — the implementation is shipped and verified.
- Do NOT write an integration test that spawns a live parent, attaches tmux, or drives the deacon patrol loop — the function under test is pure and must be tested with no live parent.
- Do NOT test the filesystem write of synthesis.md or the review-status transition — those are the call site's concern, out of scope for this pure-function unit.
- Do NOT touch convoy-revival umbrella #1982 (making convoy review opt-in).

autoDecisions.summary: Re-scoped (operator-approved) from 'implement deterministic synthesis' to 'add the missing AC4 regression test', because discovery found the implementation already shipped+verified on origin/main (bb57e9f16).
autoDecisions.rationale: Finalizing an implement-the-fix plan would spawn a work agent to re-implement shipped, verified, critical pipeline code — pure waste and a regression risk. The only acceptance criterion still unmet is AC4, the unit test.

autoDecisions.summary: Test the PURE exported function synthesizeReviewFromReports directly (no filesystem, no live parent), rather than the deacon patrol call site.
autoDecisions.rationale: The function takes report bodies in and returns {verdict, topBlocker, body} with no side effects, so testing it directly satisfies AC4's 'without any live parent' requirement with the smallest, most reliable test. The call-site behavior (write synthesis.md, transition status, kill parent) is already exercised in production and out of scope for the unit.

items.title: Add regression test for synthesizeReviewFromReports (deterministic deacon-side synthesis)

narrative.Action: Create a new vitest file src/lib/cloister/__tests__/deacon-review-synthesis.test.ts. (1) Copy the vi.mock() import scaffold verbatim from the sibling src/lib/cloister/__tests__/deacon-stash-janitor.test.ts (its lines 1-138, ~20 mocks) so that `import { synthesizeReviewFromReports } from '../deacon.js';` loads with no module-load side effects. Also import `type ReviewSubRole` from '../review-monitor.js'. (2) Add a fixture helper that builds a report `{ subRole, path, body }` whose body contains a `## Findings` section — `None` for clean, `### ! <title>` or `### ⊗ <title>` for a blocker, plain `### <title>` for an advisory note (findBlockingFindings at deacon.ts:5926 only inspects the `## Findings` section; a body without it counts as clean). (3) Assert the four acceptance criteria below by calling synthesizeReviewFromReports({ issueId: 'PAN-1864', reviewDir: '/tmp/x', reports }) and checking the returned verdict, topBlocker, and body. The function is pure — no filesystem, no tmux, no live parent. Do NOT modify any production code; the synthesis implementation already shipped on main (bb57e9f16). Run the focused vitest path and confirm green before signaling done.

items.title: Given 4 reports (one per sub-role) each with a '## Findings' section of 'None', synthesizeReviewFromReports returns verdict 'passed' and a body containing the line '## Verdict: APPROVED' — with no filesystem and no live parent.

Given 4 reports where exactly one (correctness) has '### ! Off-by-one in slice' under '## Findings' and the other three are clean, synthesizeReviewFromReports returns verdict 'blocked', topBlocker equal to '[correctness] Off-by-one in slice', and a body containing 'CHANGES REQUESTED'.
Given a report whose '## Findings' contains only advisory '###' headings without a leading '!' or '⊗', synthesizeReviewFromReports returns verdict 'passed' (advisory notes never block).
Given a report with a '### ⊗ <title>' heading under '## Findings', synthesizeReviewFromReports returns verdict 'blocked' (the ⊗ glyph blocks identically to '!').
